method: blind independent enumeration by a fresh subagent given only the verbatim README:1-70 text (no gold-set / extraction access), to measure agent-extraction false negatives (recall). See recall-probe-readme-1-70.md.

claims.assertion: Cautilus keeps agent and workflow behavior honest while prompts keep changing.
Cautilus is a repo-local contract layer for agent and workflow behavior evaluation.
Cautilus lets you define the behavior you are trying to protect once.
Cautilus verifies that the defined behavior survives prompt, skill, and wrapper changes.
Cautilus discovers declared behavior claims worth proving from selected source docs.
Cautilus verifies curated claims through bounded evaluation packets.
Cautilus improves behavior with budgeted improvement once the proof surface is honest.
Cautilus ships as a standalone binary plus Cautilus Agent.
A host repo can install Cautilus without copying another scaffold first.
Agents are first-class users of the product surface.
Commands should emit durable packets with enough state for the next agent to resume, not only terminal prose for a human operator.
Cautilus installs as a machine-level binary.
Cautilus's agent-facing surface is intentionally repo-local.
The binary is shared across repos.
The Cautilus Agent surface, adapter wiring, prompts, and instruction-routing surface are not shared across repos.
The Cautilus Agent surface and related surfaces stay checked into each host repo.
Evaluation behavior remains reproducible.
Evaluation behavior remains reviewable.
Evaluation behavior remains owned by the repo that declares it.
The current external-adoption slice is eval-only.
The broader claim, improve, live app-runner, and review-learning contracts are still being rewritten.
Host repos can use `cautilus evaluate fixture`.
Host repos can use `cautilus evaluate observation`.
Host repos can use post-run `cautilus evaluate skill-experiment`.
These evaluate commands work with checked-in fixtures, host-owned adapters, preserved task packets, and the current evaluation and skill-experiment report packets.
`skill-experiment compare` compares host-preserved baseline and variant outputs.
`skill-experiment compare` does not clone, install, or execute skills.
Claim discovery automation, improve automation, live `eval` app-runner workflows, and review-learning packet capture or selected-packet summaries are opt-in product slices until the rewrite closes.
Cautilus is not for repos that only need deterministic lint, unit, or type checks and do not have an evaluator-dependent behavior surface.
`cautilus doctor --repo-root . --next-action` emits a packet describing the next setup action to perform.
`cautilus doctor --repo-root .` reports readiness plus a `first_bounded_run` once setup is complete.
docs/specs/index.spec.md is the top-level "proven on itself" apex and the specdown entry.
The user and maintainer spec indexes that docs/specs/index.spec.md links to remain the curated claim source of truth.
Raw `discover claims` packets remain the high-recall, source-ref-backed proof-planning input, not the primary document a user should review.

The Cautilus Agent curates the discover-claims packet against the repo: reducing false positives, raising likely missing public promises, and separating in-scope discovery bugs from out-of-scope narrative gaps.
The public website report is generated from the claim spec tree.
Host repos do not need the website renderer before Cautilus can inspect readiness, claims, evals, or improvement work.
Each claim page pairs a bounded product promise with executable evidence or an explicit evidence gap.

enumeratorNotes: Line 4 split into 3 (repo-local contract layer / define once / verify survives changes). Line 6 split into the 3 connected jobs. Line 10 split into machine-level-binary + repo-local-agent-surface. Line 13 split into checked-in fact + 3 conferred properties (reproducible/reviewable/owned). Line 17 split into eval-only boundary + under-rewrite. Line 18 split into 3 command-availability + 1 conditions. Line 19 split positive compare + negative no-clone/install/execute. Lines 24-26/28 treated as audience framing (non-claims); line 30 included because 'Not for' asserts a scope boundary. Line 36 'native macOS/Linux' treated as prerequisite (non-claim).
